Title: Naringenin chalcone carbon double-bond reductases mediate dihydrochalcone biosynthesis in apple leaves

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential roles in plant defense and human health benefits; phlorizin affects glucose absorption and excretion and has potential use in treatment of type 2 diabetes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glycosylates phloretin to stable phlorizin in planta; used to trap and quantify DHC production in transient assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPGT1 was co-expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays to convert phloretin into stable phlorizin. 2. This enabled detection of DHC production by MdNCR genes in planta. 3. MdPGT1 itself was not altered by NCR RNAi in apple leaves.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling by HPLC/UPLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a core non-enzyme-support/storage-related focal gene repeatedly used for DHC pathway testing, though not the primary discovery target of this paper.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics using Apple Multidimensional Omics Database; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: MdPGT1 downregulation referenced as causing severe developmental abnormalities in prior work
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with NCR to stabilize/accumulate DHC glycosides in metabolic engineering

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid side branch leading to dihydrochalcones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the carbon-carbon double bond of naringenin chalcone to form phloretin using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate aglycone precursor to phlorizin and other DHC glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the missing key enzyme required for DHC production in apple leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to defense against pests and diseases; phlorizin has antidiabetic relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes DHC accumulation; transient expression produced very high phlorizin after co-expression with MdPGT1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNCR1a was identified by comparative transcriptomics as highly expressed in DHC-rich apple leaves. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana produced very high phlorizin levels when paired with pathway support constructs. 3. NCR RNAi in apple reduced foliar DHCs by ~85% to 95%, supporting NCR1 function in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RNAi knockdown in apple; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdNCR1a-c share >98% amino acid identity and identical substrate-binding pocket features; likely functionally redundant in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; undetectable in ripe fruit; highest in very young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; undetectable in ripe fruit; highest in very young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf developmental stage; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics from apple leaf and fruit; metabolomics/LC-MS/HPLC/UPLC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for increasing or engineering DHC content in apple and other fruits; useful for biopharming and fermentation platforms

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid side branch leading to dihydrochalcones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Efficiently reduces naringenin chalcone to phloretin using NADPH; also weakly reduces p-coumaroyl-CoA to dihydro-p-coumaroyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate aglycone precursor to phlorizin and other DHC glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme for DHC biosynthesis in apple leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to defense against pests and diseases; phlorizin has antidiabetic relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes DHC accumulation and directly catalyzes phloretin formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant MdNCR1b efficiently converted naringenin chalcone to phloretin in vitro with NADPH preference. 2. It also showed weaker activity toward p-coumaroyl-CoA and several other α,β-unsaturated substrates. 3. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana yielded very high phlorizin accumulation, and apple NCR RNAi strongly reduced DHC levels in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS product identification; kinetic analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RNAi knockdown in apple
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Km for naringenin chalcone 9.9 ± 2.6 µM; Km for NADPH 23.1 ± 9.4 µM; catalytic efficiency 18.5 s^-1·mM^-1 for naringenin chalcone; broad but selective reductase substrate scope
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Represented within MdNCR1 expression: highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; very low to absent in mature fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Represented within MdNCR1 expression: highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; very low to absent in mature fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Active from 25 to 55°C with optimum 30 to 40°C; kinetic assays optimized at pH 3.0; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptomics; LC-MS; GC-MS; HPLC; UPLC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of phlorizin, trilobatin, and other DHCs in plants or fermentation systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduction of NCRs redirected flux to flavonol glycosides and lowered total phenolics overall

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid side branch leading to dihydrochalcones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces naringenin chalcone to phloretin using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate aglycone precursor to phlorizin and other DHC glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the missing key enzyme required for DHC production in apple leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to defense against pests and diseases; phlorizin has antidiabetic relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes DHC accumulation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNCR1c belongs to the apple-specific NCR1 clade identified from leaf-enriched transcriptomics. 2. Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana produced very high phlorizin levels comparable to MdNCR1a and MdNCR1b. 3. NCR knockdown in apple confirms the NCR1 clade is essential for high DHC accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RNAi knockdown in apple; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not biochemically characterized separately because MdNCR1a-c showed essentially identical activity and identical binding pocket residues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Represented within MdNCR1 expression: highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; absent in ripe fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Represented within MdNCR1 expression: highest in leaves, stems, roots, and young fruit; absent in ripe fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for DHC biofortification and synthetic biology

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Naringenin chalcone carbon double-bond reductase-like protein
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid side branch leading to dihydrochalcones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Weakly reduces naringenin chalcone to support lower phloretin/phlorizin production in planta
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate aglycone precursor to phlorizin but with weaker activity than NCR1 proteins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as major control point; likely weaker auxiliary contributor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to defense and human health-related DHC content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces moderate phlorizin in transient assays; may contribute to low DHC production in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNCR101 is closely related to the NCR1 clade but less active in transient assays. 2. It produced >20-fold more phlorizin than the GUS control in Nicotiana benthamiana, but far less than MdNCR1a-c. 3. Because it is expressed at low levels in fruit, it may contribute to residual DHC production there.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; localization assay; comparative expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted substrate-binding pocket differs at 7 of 15 residues from NCR1 proteins, likely explaining weaker activity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression in all tissues including fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in all tissues including fruit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; roots; stems; young fruit; possibly fruit at low level
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for fruit DHC engineering where weak constitutive activity is desired

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces p-coumaroyl-CoA to dihydro-p-coumaroyl-CoA in vitro but does not reduce naringenin chalcone to phloretin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies proposed precursor pool for an alternative route to DHCs, but not supported as major leaf pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not supported for high DHC production in apple leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to defense and health-related DHC traits only in specific tissues if alternative pathway operates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation did not reduce foliar phlorizin in apple leaves, indicating it is not responsible for high leaf DHCs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHCDBR had been proposed to support DHC biosynthesis via p-coumaroyl-CoA reduction. 2. RNAi downregulation of MdHCDBR together with related ARL genes in apple leaves did not reduce phlorizin content. 3. Recombinant MdHCDBR reduced p-coumaroyl-CoA but not naringenin chalcone, ruling it out as the missing leaf NCR activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in apple; RT-qPCR; HPLC; recombinant enzyme assay with LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive for RNAi construct; CaMV 35S constitutive in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Remains a possible contributor to fruit DHC production via alternative pathway, but not to high leaf DHC accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less suitable than NCR1 for engineering high foliar DHC production; may still be relevant for alternative pathway studies in fruit
